name: Pinecone
what_it_does: Managed vector database for storing and querying high-dimensional embeddings at scale, enabling semantic search and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for AI applications.
use_cases: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) — store document embeddings and retrieve semantically relevant chunks for LLM context
Semantic search over large text corpora with k-NN and ANN algorithms
Long-term agent memory with semantic retrieval across sessions
Multimodal search over image and text embeddings
not_for: Exact keyword search or BM25 ranking (use Elasticsearch or Typesense)
Relational or structured data queries (use a SQL database)
Small datasets where SQLite with pgvector suffices
Cost-sensitive workloads at high scale (vector DBs can be expensive)
best_when: You need a fully managed, production-grade vector store for RAG or semantic search and want minimal infrastructure management with a developer-friendly API.
avoid_when: Your retrieval needs are purely keyword-based, your dataset is tiny, or you need self-hosted open-source control.

auth.notes: Single API key per project. Keys are scoped to a Pinecone project. Role-based access control (RBAC) available on enterprise plans with organization-level key management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 1 index (serverless), 2GB storage, 100k query units/month on free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Serverless: ~$0.04/1M read units, ~$0.05/1M write units; Standard pods from $0.096/hour
pricing.notes: Serverless pricing is consumption-based and unpredictable at scale. Pod-based pricing is more predictable. Free tier is genuinely useful for prototyping.

performance.rate_limits: Serverless: varies by plan; Standard: 1000 upserts/second, unlimited queries

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include status code, error type, and message. SDK surfaces underlying gRPC/REST errors. Clear distinction between client and server errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Upsert operations are idempotent by vector ID. Upserting the same ID twice updates the vector rather than duplicating it — safe for agent retries.
known_agent_gotchas: Index creation is asynchronous — must poll for READY state before querying
Serverless indexes have eventual consistency — freshly upserted vectors may not appear immediately
Namespace must match between upsert and query — agents that omit namespace will search the default namespace only
Free tier allows only 1 serverless index — plan for this in multi-tenant architectures
Metadata filtering has size limits (~40KB per vector) — large metadata payloads cause silent truncation or errors
gRPC SDK requires separate install (`pinecone[grpc]`) — REST SDK is default

security_score_components.security_notes: API key authentication, single key per project. SOC2 Type II, HIPAA available. Encryption at rest and in transit. No granular scope control — all-or-nothing API key access.
